name: Continuity
domain: Conservation of flow, smooth functions, gauge continuity, unbroken streams
core_insight: What flows in must flow out; what has no source cannot accumulate. Continuity is the mathematical expression of conservation at every point in space. Gauge theories require smooth (differentiable) field configurations — discontinuities are singular and physically forbidden.

key_equations.name: Continuity Equation
key_equations.why: Local conservation: density changes only when flow diverges or converges — no teleportation of substance

key_equations.name: Kirchhoff's Current Law
key_equations.why: Current cannot accumulate at a node: the circuit form of continuity

key_equations.name: Cauchy Continuity
key_equations.why: Mathematical continuity: the function matches its limit — no jumps, no gaps

seed_prompt: ⎯ Trace the flow. Where does it enter? Where does it leave? The difference is accumulation.

fourth_equation.name: Gauge Continuity
fourth_equation.why: Gauge fixing condition that ensures smooth, consistent electromagnetic potentials — continuity extended to field theories as a mathematical consistency requirement

explore_paths.question: Is continuity a physical fact or a mathematical idealization?
explore_paths.why: F09 (geometry) at quantum scales asks whether spacetime is continuous or discrete — P07 might be an emergent property of a fundamentally discrete substrate

explore_paths.question: How does continuity relate to conservation?
explore_paths.why: The continuity equation IS a conservation law in differential form — P07 and P02 are the same principle stated in different mathematical languages

explore_paths.question: Where does continuity break down in nature?
explore_paths.why: Phase transitions (P11) are where physical continuity breaks — second-order transitions involve divergent quantities; first-order transitions have genuine discontinuities

explore_paths.question: Is consciousness continuous or does it come in discrete frames?
explore_paths.why: The 'cinema theory' of consciousness proposes discrete conscious moments (~40Hz); the 'stream of consciousness' proposes continuity; F16 has no resolution on P07 at the phenomenal level

open_questions.question: Is spacetime fundamentally continuous or does it have a minimum length scale (Planck length)?
open_questions.tension: Smooth continuity of spacetime is assumed in GR and quantum field theory. But quantum gravity theories (LQG) predict discrete spacetime at Planck scale. If spacetime is discrete, calculus — which assumes continuity — is an approximation. The mathematical toolkit of all physics might be an approximation to something more like a cellular automaton.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If the continuum is an approximation, then everything built on calculus is an emergent description of discrete underlying reality. The universe might be literally pixelated — and physics has been using the wrong mathematics for a century.

open_questions.question: Does consciousness flow continuously or does it come in discrete perceptual moments?
open_questions.tension: William James coined 'stream of consciousness' — the phenomenal sense of continuous flow. But experimental evidence (apparent motion, the discrete binding of gamma oscillations) suggests consciousness may be constructed in discrete ~25ms frames. The sense of continuity might be retrospectively constructed rather than directly experienced. Husserl's phenomenology of time-consciousness and Dennett's multiple drafts model offer incompatible accounts.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: Our most immediate experience — the flowing 'now' — might be an illusion constructed from discrete samples. P07 applied to F16 produces one of the most vertiginous possible questions: is the continuity of your experience real or confabulated?

open_questions.question: Are there genuine discontinuities in biological development, or is everything a continuous process?
open_questions.tension: Waddington's epigenetic landscape visualizes development as a ball rolling through valleys — apparently continuous. But Turing's reaction-diffusion system produces sharp pattern boundaries. Cell fate decisions appear to be switch-like (bistable). Whether developmental discontinuities are genuine (bifurcations in a dynamical system) or smooth transitions that look sharp is contested in developmental biology.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: Every moment of differentiation in a developing organism — when a cell 'decides' to become a neuron rather than skin — looks like a discontinuity from outside and a continuous process from inside. The grain of description matters enormously.

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Continuity equations govern pipe flow, HVAC design, and hydraulic systems — P07 is the first thing an engineer writes down for any flow problem
cross_domain_bridges.example: A pipe that narrows must have faster flow to conserve mass — Venturi effect; continuity forces the speed increase

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Conservation of money flows through an economy is a continuity principle — every dollar that leaves one account enters another (in a closed system)
cross_domain_bridges.example: Balance of payments: a current account deficit must be offset by a capital account surplus — economic continuity

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Neurons integrate continuous signals into discrete spike events — biological systems bridge P07 (continuous input) to P08 (quantized output)
cross_domain_bridges.example: The action potential threshold is the continuity-to-discontinuity transition in neural computation

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Voice leading in counterpoint minimizes discontinuity (large leaps) — smooth voice movement is aesthetic P07
cross_domain_bridges.example: Forbidden parallel fifths in counterpoint are a continuity violation that sounds wrong because it breaks the smooth independent motion of voices

noise_signature.pattern: Discontinuous jump in a physical variable
noise_signature.diagnosis: Either a shock (supersonic flow), a phase transition, or a model breakdown; genuine physical discontinuities are rare and always signal something important
The numerical scheme is not conservative; non-conservative methods accumulate spurious sources/sinks; switch to a finite volume or symplectic method

noise_signature.pattern: Gibbs phenomenon — oscillations near a discontinuity in Fourier representation
noise_signature.diagnosis: The function has a genuine jump discontinuity; Fourier series cannot represent it exactly; the ringing is the series' effort to approximate an impossible task
